prompt: A tightly zoomed-in comparison image demonstrating fake pixel-art rendering, showing a low-resolution-looking scene enlarged on screen with a thin bright blue square grid overlay across the entire frame. The composition is an abstract close crop of a pixel-art-style environment: sandy tan and ochre blocky forms on the left and center, a dark navy and black stepped diagonal mass on the right, muted olive-green and slate-blue shapes along the upper background, and a few lighter golden highlights. Every edge looks staircase-like and blocky at first glance, but the colors softly blur and blend within the apparent squares instead of aligning perfectly to the grid, creating the impression of pixel art that breaks down under inspection. Include a small white crosshair cursor near the upper left quadrant to suggest image analysis in an editor. The overall look should feel like a screenshot from a graphics program or social media demonstration about how AI can imitate retro game art aesthetics without producing true grid-perfect pixel art, with no text labels visible.
